title: SHOPPING Wheres the best place to find Eva merch
display_title: SHOPPING Wheres the best place to find Eva merch
wikitext:
[]: [SHOPPING] Where's the best place to find Eva merch?             Quote Post by SAWgunner » Thu Mar 05, 2009 6:51 am When I got into Eva the first time, it seemed like every anime webstore had about 50 gajillion different related items, from posters to life-size creeper love pillows of your favorite character. Now though, the pickins seem a lot more slim. All I really want are some sweet posters or wallscrolls but every place I go to has the same two or three items that I don't want. Where should I go to get a better selection? Please don't say evastore.jp, because if you do then I'll start thinking about all the sweet jank that I can never have and start cutting myself.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 05, 2009 8:34 am Sounds like you didn't find a proxy yet.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Mar 05, 2009 10:22 am I love JPQueen. http://www.jpqueen.com/ Mandarake is also useful. http://ekizo.mandarake.co.jp/shop/en/

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Fri Mar 06, 2009 2:54 pm is it possible to send it to yourself in Japan if your visiting as i may in the summer for 2.0?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Mar 06, 2009 5:27 pm Axell wrote: is it possible to send it to yourself in Japan if your visiting as i may in the summer for 2.0?

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sun Mar 08, 2009 12:53 pm Axell wrote: is it possible to send it to yourself in Japan if your visiting as i may in the summer for 2.0?

[]: Deputy Service             Quote Post by XX » Sun Mar 08, 2009 4:00 pm SAWgunner wrote: Please don't say evastore.jp , because if you do then I'll start thinking about all the sweet jank that I can never have and start cutting myself.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Tue Mar 10, 2009 8:02 am Have you used this website before (is it a scam)?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Mar 10, 2009 8:18 am Oedipusfoot wrote: Have you used this website before (is it a scam)?

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Mar 10, 2009 11:19 am The seventh layer of Hell. Eva merchandise is so... damn. I'm waiting for them to make Entry Plug Tampons and A.T. Field Condoms.

[]:             Quote Post by SAWgunner » Tue Mar 10, 2009 11:27 am I would never use an Eva condom. It would dissolve as soon as I hit Third Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr.Sexy » Wed Mar 11, 2009 4:09 am Well... It may seem silly and obvious. But some seller's on Ebay, have some very cool eva related stuff. And i having no real life... (other than collecting figurines) have spent many a couple hours. digging though ebay for good reliable seller's. If ya like i could tell you some but in case you don't need or care about my advice.(which is understandable) i won't demand you take it. All i can say is if you want some kickass Eva wall scrolls! then look up seller animecast on Ebay.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Wed Mar 11, 2009 5:39 am For rarer things and things like doujinshi, ebay proves to be very expensive. Even if you are buying from a Japanese seller, you'll still have to pay a lot. It just depends on what you're buying, I guess.

[]:             Quote Post by SAWgunner » Wed Mar 11, 2009 11:28 am Mr.Sexy wrote: Well... It may seem silly and obvious. But some seller's on Ebay, have some very cool eva related stuff. And i having no real life... (other than collecting figurines) have spent many a couple hours. digging though ebay for good reliable seller's. If ya like i could tell you some but in case you don't need or care about my advice.(which is understandable) i won't demand you take it. All i can say is if you want some kickass Eva wall scrolls! then look up seller animecast on Ebay.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Mar 11, 2009 12:02 pm Decent domestic sellers loaded with affordable (and preferably non-bootleg...) Eva swag? If such things still exist, I'm afraid that you may just have to "suck it up" and put the effort into digging around for them. Like I told you in the other thread, you could wait around forever for someone to magically materialize and point you to exactly what you want -- and, in the meantime, you could theoretically be hitting gold with a search engine.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Mar 11, 2009 4:44 pm Off the top of my head here are places I can think of to buy things from (yes I've brought something from all of them): CD Japan http://www.cdjapan.co.jp/ Play Asia http://www.play-asia.com/ Palet Mail web http://www.paletweb.com/ The Little Nook http://www.thelittlenook.com/ (Best for things like doujinshi) EDIT: I forgot this one earlier: http://www.1999.co.jp/eng/ Hobby Search (not to get confused with Hobby Link). All of these stores are nice and I haven't come across any bootlegs from these places, although with Paletweb the people seem to speak Engrish there so you need to be clear with what you want.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr.Sexy » Thu Mar 12, 2009 2:22 pm The best part about the sellers i have on Ebay is they are all store seller's with fixed prices. so no worrying bout bidding and gay crap like that. But you'd be doing yourself a great favor to look at Animecast. ;) I mean i bought an Asuka wallscroll from him for only 17.00$CDN & thats inculding shipping! And all of the wallscrolls he carries are all huge! apperantly they measure (80 x 102 cm) each. And add to that the fact that all of them are usually under 20$, And they all are the same huge size! As well they carry one's from just about every anime!(Iria Zeriam Wallscroll ) so it's total win win in general. srsly check the store out you won't be disspointed!!!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 12, 2009 4:08 pm Mr.Sexy wrote: I mean i bought an Asuka wallscroll from him for only 17.00$CDN & thats inculding shipping!

[]:             Quote Post by Mr.Sexy » Thu Mar 12, 2009 4:53 pm Reichu wrote: Mr.Sexy wrote: I mean i bought an Asuka wallscroll from him for only 17.00$CDN & thats inculding shipping!

[]: Looking for Eva rare figures             Quote Post by evalienadia » Mon Apr 06, 2009 1:57 am I am looking for a shop that sells Eva rare figures in the U.S. or that can deliver internationally. I found this site http://www.geocities.co.jp/bujacity/ but I am not sure if this site is reliable. If someone has ever bought something from this site will you tell me how it was ?? They say they have 5.2 inch Ayanami figures and I really want to buy it. Only what matters is if the site is reliable. Evalie-nadia
